title: Am I the only one who liked Death
display_title: Am I the only one who liked Death
wikitext:
[]: Am I the only one who liked Death?             Quote Post by chaosakita » Fri Jul 26, 2013 5:10 pm I know Death gets knocked off for being a clip show, but I think it's a very well-done clip show. I think the presentation is terrific, and I love the quartet theme. I also can't believe Canon in D was never used in the original series (or was it). I know it's a pretty cheesy song, but I still love it all the same. In general, the soundtrack is great.

[]:             Quote Post by Atropos » Fri Jul 26, 2013 5:12 pm I agree. It's not strictly necessary, but it's far from bad.

[]:             Quote Post by Kendrix » Fri Jul 26, 2013 6:15 pm I also think its brutally underappreciated and under analyzed. The way it combines/cuts the scenes is just serious art, the arrangement itself/combinations just give you a lot to think about... I just wish there was a way to see the "final version" on the internets which never made it to the states, the one that conjoins Death with EoE with cut live-action material... kinda like it was intended if they had finished shit in time. (If it actually IS somewhere, probably some asian site that can't be googled because runes, pm me. ) Just think of the most blatant things, like: - Misato's little logs about episode 4. Besides her being very efficient at ignoring the obvious elephant-sized problem, hearing that the whole Shamshel deal affected Shinji to the degree that he was notably physically rundown at the end of his "road trip" does show just how badly the experience hit him. - ReiI's Kaworu vision. Since she ended up in instrumentality/appears in EoE, is he her transistion guide or something? It's also indentical to that flash of him we see in combinastion with Rei III's deliberations on him - so it isn't just emphasizing the red eyes, there might be some half-concious recognition which adds to why ReiIII is so suspicious about him... Not even these ever get adressed in FFs or shit.

[]:             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Fri Jul 26, 2013 6:42 pm Death is great. Anytime I wanna watch EoE, I'll pop in Death first. It's wonderfully high art and does really well at reminding the viewer of the thematics from the TV show before heading into the series' theatrical finally. I would never watch it as a stand-alone feature, though.

[]:             Quote Post by peripateia » Fri Jul 26, 2013 7:14 pm @ Chaosakita you are definitely not alone! The quartet rehearsal scene in the high school auditorium provided an unexpected perspective of the eva pilots as young students. That these kids could savor music right before the start of the apocalypse, was beautiful to me. Looking back on it after 8 years, it's a cliche alright. But it's an endearing one: this is a very humble, humanizing moment in eva, when four gifted children come together to make ancient music for the joy of it. The fact that this scene could never have happened in the 'canon reality' of NGE makes it all the more provocative, like a false memory or lucid dream. The scene also symbolically unites the separate strands of the individual Childrens' storylines together into a single concerted effort, before dispelling Shinji once more toward the lonely exit. It is succinct summary, meta-commentary, bittersweet premonition and expressive music all in one. Plus, it was the first time I'd heard Pachebel's Canon, I was in absolute awe by the end of it...

[]:             Quote Post by chaosakita » Fri Jul 26, 2013 9:42 pm Kendrix wrote: I just wish there was a way to see the "final version" on the internets which never made it to the states, the one that conjoins Death with EoE with cut live-action material... kinda like it was intended if they had finished shit in time. (If it actually IS somewhere, probably some asian site that can't be googled because runes, pm me. )

[]:             Quote Post by SEELE-01 » Sat Jul 27, 2013 12:40 am How interesting... I had read the transcript of that scene but never actually see in... WTF does it mean Is it a variation of the school-comedy/hallucination scene of NGE-26?

[]:             Quote Post by riffraff11235 » Sat Jul 27, 2013 12:43 am I liked Death, but most of my initiated friends found it useless.

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Sat Jul 27, 2013 1:49 am SEELE-01 wrote: Is it a variation of the school-comedy/hallucination scene of NGE-26?

[]: Re: Am I the only one who liked Death?             Quote Post by Sachi » Sat Jul 27, 2013 2:53 am I love Death. Although I tend to neglect my copy more than the rest in my Eva collection, the way it's structured and presented summarizes the series plot and themes quite well, without actually reducing itself to an episode-by-episode recount of what's happened. In fact, I'd say the way it presents some the characterization is more effective than the series proper! Especially so if using it to warm up for EoE. chaosakita wrote: I also can't believe Canon in D was never used in the original series (or was it). I know it's a pretty cheesy song, but I still love it all the same.

[]: Re: Am I the only one who liked Death?             Quote Post by Shinoyami65 » Sat Jul 27, 2013 4:36 am chaosakita wrote: I also can't believe Canon in D was never used in the original series (or was it). I know it's a pretty cheesy song, but I still love it all the same. In general, the soundtrack is great.

[]: Re: Am I the only one who liked Death?             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Sat Jul 27, 2013 5:51 am Shinoyami65 wrote: Has it been subbed?

[]:             Quote Post by Shinoyami65 » Sat Jul 27, 2013 5:56 am Are the subs of the same quality as the Platinum subs or are they more like the original ADV subs?

[]:             Quote Post by Giji Shinka » Sat Jul 27, 2013 6:40 am I really liked death (rebirth is useless without saying), especially how it was directed. (Great source of inspiration for me)

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Sat Jul 27, 2013 7:34 am I like the framing elements of the string quartet that can't possibly have ever actually happened, especially the very classy transition of the slow sunset fade directly into Shinji's 2nd suicide attempt at the beginning of EoE. Asside from that though I dislike Death as it's the source of nearly all the retcon stuff that would eventually be threaded back into the DC episodes. People forget that when death was released a large portion of the clip show elements were actually entirely new, most of it aimed squarely at changing Asuka and her relationship with Shinji. If you followed the same path I first did, watching the broadcast version of the series then skipping Death (I heard it was just a recap clip show) and going straight to EoE, you'd see just how extreme a shift it really is.

[]:             Quote Post by tomrule123 » Sat Jul 27, 2013 2:43 pm I don't hate it, but I don't love it. For me, it's okay.

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Sat Jul 27, 2013 4:30 pm Giji Shinka wrote: I really liked death (rebirth is useless without saying), especially how it was directed. (Great source of inspiration for me)

[]:             Quote Post by Giji Shinka » Sat Jul 27, 2013 4:42 pm Okay, okay, the song was the ONLY thing that justified rebirth.

[]:             Quote Post by Katana » Sat Jul 27, 2013 4:51 pm Bagheera wrote: Ahem. Rebirth gave us Tamashii No Rufuran, one of the better songs in NGE's music library. That song alone justifies Rebirth's existence.

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Sat Jul 27, 2013 4:55 pm Katana wrote: Does this play too? I never watched Death & Rebirth.
